subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Muscle Repair After Injury
description: A micro-lesson that makes Muscle Repair After Injury usable.

hook.text: When muscle fibers tear, satellite cells activate like sleeping backup generators.

buildup.text: Muscle injuries range from mild strains to complete tears that require surgery.

discovery.text: Satellite cells fuse with damaged fibers, donating their nuclei to rebuild muscle.

twist.text: Scar tissue in muscle is less elastic—making re-injury more likely in the same spot.

climax.text: Gradual rehabilitation stretches scar tissue and rebuilds strength safely.

punchline.text: Muscles heal, but they never quite forget where they broke.

quiz.question: What special cells help repair damaged muscle fibers?
